grader.method: Blind review of every complete canonical reader snapshot in displayed order, checking factual and arithmetic correctness, narrative continuity, prerequisite and objective coverage, commit-before-reveal transfer, deterministic feedback, self-containment, and evidence boundaries without viewing prior grades.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The home page presents a six-territory path from embodied task contracts and state estimation through data, multimodal policies, control, operations, and a final research study.
wholePageReview.feedback: The sequence is coherent, prerequisites and cumulative capstones are visible, and the page clearly separates self-contained browser fixtures from optional external execution. Its course-scale scope, time expectations, and progression give a beginner an accurate map without disguising simulations as robot evidence.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson builds an embodied task contract around body, sensors, actuators, feedback, and a sense-decide-act-check loop, then applies it to a mug-placement trace.
wholePageReview.feedback: The current camera, force, displacement, and human-entry cases make continue, retry, and stop decisions observable, while the deterministic code and changed stale-camera case preserve the same authority logic. Both objectives are taught, rehearsed before reveal, and transferred to a new person-path case with specific local feedback.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page turns observations and actions into typed interface contracts covering shapes, units, frames, time, validity, bounds, hold behavior, and saturation.
wholePageReview.feedback: The seven-degree-of-freedom example and normalized-action calculation correctly distinguish requested from applied motion, including rotation and clipping. Non-finite and dimension failures plus the two-robot changed case force learners to reason from the contract rather than memorize field names.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson explains transform direction, composition, inversion, timestamps, and staleness as one contract for placing measurements in a common frame and time.
wholePageReview.feedback: The notation and one-dimensional 0.2 + 0.5 + 1.0 trace are correct, and the 120 ms motion error and 40 ms rejection threshold connect timing to spatial error. Direction, inverse, stale-data, and nonmatching-chain checks provide meaningful changed cases with no missing prerequisite.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page develops belief state from observation history and applied actions, then adds synchronized evidence, uncertainty, and attachment validity under occlusion.
wholePageReview.feedback: The cube propagation from 0.40 to 0.46 and variance growth from 0.001 to 0.0022 are internally consistent and correctly bounded by grasp evidence. The no-grasp, contradiction, paired-occlusion, and dropout cases expose why hidden state is a qualified estimate rather than remembered truth.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This capstone asks learners to implement a versioned reset-and-step task interface with validation, requested/applied actions, replay, failure injection, and explicit evidence.
wholePageReview.feedback: The stale and future timestamp rejection leaves state untouched, while the repaired 0.07 request clips to 0.03 with an inspectable receipt. Staged instructions, local tests, smoke verification, rubric, downloadable artifact, and simulator boundary make the project executable and honestly scoped.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson assembles camera, joint, force, and depth readings into a synchronized sensor packet governed by age, shape, unit, frame, and calibration checks.
wholePageReview.feedback: The 18/4/7 ms valid readings, 61 ms stale depth, negative-age camera, and revision mismatch sharply distinguish freshness from mere availability. The executable validation and saturation/common-clock transfers sustain the mechanism from explanation through assessment.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page separates camera intrinsics from extrinsics and treats calibration as a fitted, held-out, versioned estimate whose uncertainty affects action clearance.
wholePageReview.feedback: The 0.004 versus 0.006 acceptance gate, post-change 0.011 invalidation, and combined 0.010 uncertainty calculation are correct and decision-relevant. Learners must predict the held-out failure and connect calibration revision drift to downstream motion rather than accept fit error alone.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson defines a query-driven object representation with identity, pose uncertainty, frame, extent, time, visibility, and relations.
wholePageReview.feedback: The cup example correctly shows why a 0.06 center distance fails a 0.05 reach rule while an extent-aware 0.09 clearance can pass a different geometric query. Permutation invariance, stable IDs, and unknown-versus-free space are carried into the lab and transfer check without overclaiming a universal representation.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page traces prediction, innovation gating, Kalman-style update, missed observations, coverage, likelihood, and identity recovery as one tracking workflow.
wholePageReview.feedback: The innovation variance 0.05, squared Mahalanobis distance 0.20, gain 0.8, posterior 0.48 with variance 0.008, and dropout update are arithmetically sound. Coverage and NLL are kept separate from identity recovery, while the common-bias transfer prevents learners from treating fusion as automatic truth.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This project integrates packet validation, transforms, covariance propagation, association, tracking, and truth-isolated evaluation into a reproducible state estimator.
wholePageReview.feedback: The 90-degree point and covariance rotation are correct, and the three-packet recovery trace makes freshness and recovery observable. The staged build, paired sensor degradation, failure rows, local verifier, and machine-readable reference satisfy the full capstone contract without relying on an external judge.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson frames demonstrations as provenance-rich transition records containing requested and applied actions, timing, operator/session identity, corrections, failures, and usage rights.
wholePageReview.feedback: The joystick-to-4 cm request, 3 cm applied limit, and 70 ms latency correctly establish which action owns the transition. Operator and easy-start confounding plus explicit retention of failed demonstrations make the data-collection lesson causal, inspectable, and transferable.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page constructs trajectory datasets from versioned episodes and steps, with checksums, lineage, rights, and group-level splitting before window extraction.
wholePageReview.feedback: The E7/E8 split and labeled 98-versus-74 teaching fixture clearly demonstrate how random windows can leak episode identity without pretending those values are measured performance. The executable source episode-step overlap check catches the injected E1 row and supports a genuine changed-case assessment.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson audits robot data through independent schema, timing, duplication, coverage, and rights gates rather than a single quality score.
wholePageReview.feedback: The 12 recoveries in 10,000 rows correctly yield 0.12 percent and reveal thin critical-slice coverage despite dataset size. The 92-to-71 deduplication fixture and independently blocking missing-consent case give learners concrete, locally checked reasons to reject superficially large data.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page specifies absolute, delta, joint, and task-space actions with units, frames, rates, gripper semantics, chunk horizon, execution prefix, and masks.
wholePageReview.feedback: The four-step chunk at 20 Hz correctly spans 0.2 seconds and the two-valid-step mask is explicit. Incompatible absolute/delta and reversed gripper meanings, plus executable dimension and unit rejection, make the representation boundary observable before policy training.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This capstone builds a behavior-cloning pipeline with dataset lineage, masks, scaling, a tiny fit, resume evidence, critical slices, and closed-loop evaluation.
wholePageReview.feedback: The two-centimeter intervention and explicitly explained zero-based support-departure indices make compounding error traceable rather than rhetorical. Full project stages, checkpoint and artifact contracts, paired evaluation, deterministic verification, and bounded claims support both implementation and transfer.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson parses instructions into referents, relations, goals, and constraints, then separates semantic confidence from physical affordance.
wholePageReview.feedback: The two-candidate case correctly rejects both objects under a 0.7 joint threshold even though each is strong on only one axis. The ambiguous pronoun transfer requires asking or abstaining, preserving the distinction between language plausibility and safe executable grounding.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page turns vision, proprioception, language, and special tokens into one masked multimodal sequence for policy encoding.
wholePageReview.feedback: The 64 + 12 + 1 + 1 token count, width 128, attention-score shape, and alternate mask shapes are consistent. The batch-two missing-proprioception example leaves 77 valid tokens, while counterfactual texture tests expose shortcut reliance rather than equating attention with causal use.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson derives causal serialization and target masks for transformer action prediction, then connects teacher forcing to closed-loop exposure bias.
wholePageReview.feedback: The instruction-observation-action sequence correctly places each action target after its observation and excludes diagonal answer-token leakage. Three valid losses, future-edit invariance, and the 95-versus-40 teacher-forced/rollout contrast form a coherent changed-case learning journey.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page explains conditional action diffusion through corruption, noise prediction, clean-action reconstruction, iterative sampling, and receding-horizon timing.
wholePageReview.feedback: The alpha 0.8 and sigma 0.6 arithmetic yields the stated noisy and reconstructed actions, with shapes and conditioning declared. Comparing 20 steps at 120 ms against five at 30 ms under a 50 ms deadline, plus stale-condition rejection, correctly ties generative flexibility to control constraints.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This capstone compares causal-transformer and diffusion vision-language-action policies under matched data, encoders, actions, updates, seeds, and evaluation.
wholePageReview.feedback: The hard gates correctly reject a 60 ms diffusion policy under a 50 ms deadline and a 20 ms transformer that ignores the blue-target instruction. Smoke/full profiles, attention evidence, future-edit invariance, paired interventions, and preserved failures make the comparison controlled and locally verifiable.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson develops proportional feedback, clipping, delay, queues, anti-windup, and bounded authority from a simple closed-loop plant.
wholePageReview.feedback: The 0.4 error, 0.8 raw command, 0.5 clip, and delayed-state outcomes are correct, and the anti-windup and oscillatory delay traces expose distinct failure mechanisms. The executable delayed queue and authority boundary carry the math into a meaningful changed case.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page uses uncertainty-penalized model predictions to rank plans, execute only a prefix, observe reality, and replan or fall back.
wholePageReview.feedback: The 8 minus 1 versus 11 minus 5 comparison is correct, while the horizon-three code shows a slip-induced replan and an independent hold when all candidates violate constraints. Rank reversal and matched planning-budget cases directly address model exploitation and unfair comparison.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson specifies hierarchical skills through typed arguments, preconditions, invariants, effects, termination, and recovery rather than opaque names.
wholePageReview.feedback: The cup-grasp timeout correctly withholds the holding effect when contact is absent, and the executable workspace violation triggers hold before later contact. Selection, argument, and execution diagnoses help learners locate the failed layer and choose a bounded recovery.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page treats sim-to-real work as parameter identification under observability limits, held-out validation, randomized support, and protected final tests.
wholePageReview.feedback: The frictionless force-acceleration mass calculation, least-squares inverse mass, held-out error, and rank-one confounding diagnosis are correct. The out-of-range friction and final-test retuning cases clearly distinguish robustness evidence from adaptive reuse of evaluation data.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This capstone integrates independent constraints, watchdog stopping, rollback, replay state, human authority, fault injection, and autonomy labeling.
wholePageReview.feedback: The 80 ms stale command is correctly replaced by hold under a 40 ms limit, rollback restores state and RNG, and the repaired fresh-count sequence is consistent with reset behavior. The project keeps watchdog, authority, resume, assisted outcomes, and residual-risk evidence separate enough for local verification.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson builds evaluation as a task-by-shift matrix with independent autonomy, assistance, safety, grounding, deadline, and uncertainty records.
wholePageReview.feedback: The Wilson intervals for 9/10 and 2/10 and the 0.55 macro average are correct, while the prose appropriately avoids treating interval nonoverlap as a hypothesis test. The correlated-frame changed case prevents inflated sample counts and reinforces the independent-unit denominator.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page distinguishes nuisance, causal, compositional, and out-of-range shifts using held groups, counterfactuals, severity curves, and calibration checks.
wholePageReview.feedback: Texture-preserving versus target-changing interventions correctly separate shortcut behavior from causal response, and the 0/2/4 cm severity results locate the failure onset. The confidence-after-collapse example and final-layout selection warning prevent aggregate robustness and validation reuse from being mistaken for generalization.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The lesson separates a window-level p99 release SLO from per-command watchdog admission, then adds provenance, incident replay, authority, and rollback compatibility.
wholePageReview.feedback: The nearest-rank 100-command fixture correctly yields p50 32 ms and p99 88 ms, blocking promotion while still admitting a current valid 32 ms command under the declared mode and holding an 88 ms one. Changed mode/provenance cases and the incompatible-schema rollback check make both operational decisions explicit without implying hardware certification.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The page defines portable smoke and full accelerator profiles with pinned environments, device receipts, paired episode contracts, stop rules, and complete external artifacts.
wholePageReview.feedback: The smoke gate recomputes checkpoint, full episode specification, action opportunity, finite-row, schema, and seed invariants rather than trusting self-reported flags. It accurately treats success rates and treatment direction as variable observations, preserves null or mixed results, and bounds hosted simulation evidence away from browser or physical-robot claims.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The final capstone first reproduces a pinned bounded proportional controller, then changes only gain from 0.35 to 0.55 under paired disturbances and hard operational gates.
wholePageReview.feedback: The raw-trace verifier independently regenerates starts and disturbances, replays clipped actions and states, rejects a finite forgery, and withholds treatment interpretation until the full seed-by-arm matrix passes. Detailed stages, local commands, hard deadline logic, evidence dossier, rubric, attempt-gated exemplar, and bounded simulator conclusion provide a complete cumulative research journey.
